unions.question: What is the main difference between a struct and a union in C?
options: A struct can store different types, a union cannot
A union can store different types, a struct cannot
In a struct, members have separate memory; in a union, members share the same memory
A struct can only store integers, a union can store any type
unions.prompt_zh: main difference between a struct and a union in C 是什麼？

unions.question:
What will the following code output?
union myUnion {
  int myNum;
  char myLetter;
};

int main() {
  union myUnion u1;
  u1.myNum = 1000;
  u1.myLetter = 'A';
  printf("%c", u1.myLetter);
  return 0;
}
unions.prompt_zh: [union] What will the following code output?union myUnion {  int myNum;  char myLetter;};int main() {  union myUnion u1;  u1.myNum = 1000;  u1.myLetter = 'A';  printf("%c", u1.myLetter);  return 0;}
